# atavola

> Group meal planning by swiping. A host picks a vibe, the AI generates four meal ideas with images and ingredient lists, and the group votes on a public link. Results re-rank live and never lock — anyone with the link keeps voting until the group lands on something.

atavola is a free, non-commercial, open-source project built at the IBM Bobathon in Zürich. It's designed to be the fastest way for 2-20 people to decide what to cook or order, in roughly 60 seconds, with no app to install and no account needed to vote.

## Key facts

- **Free** to use, no monetisation, no advertising, no third-party data sharing.
- **Account-less voting** — only the host signs in (magic-link email). Voters open a public share link.
- **Live results** — the ranking updates in real time as people swipe.
- **Saved meals** — hosts can save favourite meal cards for re-use ("My Food").
- **Built on free tiers** — Supabase, Vercel, OpenRouter free models, Pollinations + Hugging Face + Cloudflare image generation. Total infrastructure cost: $0/month at hackathon scale.

## Important pages

- [Home](https://atavola.ch/): create a session, sign in
- [About](https://atavola.ch/about): what atavola is, who built it, why
- [Privacy](https://atavola.ch/privacy): data handling — anonymised visit + login counters, no cookies, no fingerprinting
- [Terms](https://atavola.ch/terms): free-to-use, abuse policy

## How it works

1. Host signs in with a magic-link email.
2. Host enters a vibe (e.g. "Cozy Movie Night"), headcount, dietary flags. Optionally pre-selects up to 4 meals from "My Food".
3. The AI generates four meal cards (title, description, ingredients scaled to headcount, image).
4. Host shares a public link, QR code, or uses the native share sheet.
5. Voters swipe right to like, left to dislike. They can heart any card to save it to their own profile (sign-in prompt if anonymous).
6. The results screen re-ranks live as votes come in. There's no terminal "winner" — voting stays open.

## Common user intents

- "decide what to cook for dinner with friends"
- "tinder for meals"
- "group decision tool for what to eat"
- "ai meal planner for a group"
- "swipe to vote on dinner"
- "what should we order tonight"

## Contact

no-reply@atavola.ch (no-reply is intentional — write anyway).
